description: Whether the provider supports organization-level MCP configuration management, including managed server lists, allowlists, and enterprise registries.

amp.mechanism: enterprise_registry_allowlist: Amp supports organization-level MCP configuration with an enterprise registry and allowlist

claude-code.mechanism: mcp_managed_config: managed-mcp.json for exclusive control, or allowedMcpServers/deniedMcpServers in managed settings for policy-based control

cline.mechanism: Cline does not document organization-level MCP configuration management

codex.mechanism: Codex does not document organization-level MCP management

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI does not document organization-level MCP management

crush.mechanism: Crush does not document organization-level MCP management

cursor.mechanism: Cursor does not document organization-level managed MCP policy controls beyond the general hook and rule enterprise scopes.

factory-droid.mechanism: only user-level (~/.factory/mcp.json) and project-level (.factory/mcp.json) configuration scopes documented; no organization/enterprise admin layer

gemini-cli.mechanism: Gemini CLI does not document organization-level MCP management

kiro.mechanism: Kiro does not document organization-level MCP management

opencode.mechanism: Organizations can provide default MCP server configurations via a .well-known/opencode endpoint; users can override remote defaults in their local opencode.json; local config values take precedence over remote defaults

windsurf.mechanism: enterprise_whitelist_and_registry: Windsurf supports organization-level MCP server allowlists and enterprise registries

zed.mechanism: Zed does not document org-level MCP management
